articles.title: Landing astronauts on the moon 'is absolutely doable, and it's doable soon,' NASA's Artemis 2 commander says
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts said they gained a deeper understanding of their Orion capsule during their moon mission —and what they learned bodes well for future Artemis flights.

articles.title: 'That was a wonderful toilet': Artemis 2 commander defends mission's lunar loo
articles.description: Artemis 2's groundbreaking space toilet doesn't deserve the bad press it's gotten over the past two weeks, according to mission commander Reid Wiseman.

articles.title: All Points signs agreement to build payload processing facilities at KSC
articles.description: All Points Logistics has signed an agreement with NASA’s Kennedy Space Center to construct satellite processing facilities on center property. The post All Points signs agreement to build payload processing facilities at KSC appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
articles.description: The Republic of Latvia will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at 9 a.m. EDT Monday, April 20, at NASA Headquarters in Washington. articles.title: How space weather forecasting keeps astronauts (and satellites) safe
articles.description: BOULDER, Colorado – The sun’s volatile outbursts, such as storms, flares and other space weather, can cause serious harm to astronauts like the Artemis 2 crew who recently came home, and to satellites. That’s why the Space Weather Prediction Center (SWPC) in Boulder, Colorado — part of the National

articles.title: At the Edge of Light
articles.description: In this photo taken on April 6, 2026, a portion of the Moon’s far side is seen along the terminator—the boundary between lunar day and night—where low-angle sunlight casts long shadows across the surface. articles.title: Artemis 2 is a benchmark in our space exploration program — and it depends on steady NASA funding
articles.description: Now that the Artemis 2 mission has been successfully completed, it’s worth taking a look at where NASA stands on the role of humans in exploring space and what its path forward should be. articles.title: Max Space unveils new expandable space habitat for the moon and beyond: 'We need real estate that is scalable'
articles.description: "They are truly ready for long duration space flights and lunar habitation."

articles.title: A new Earthrise: An Apollo historian experiences Artemis 2
articles.description: As Artemis 2 approached, my anticipation was mixed with uncertainty. Would this new Moon mission spark the wonder and excitement I'd felt during Apollo? Those doubts didn't last long.

articles.title: Go behind the scenes of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission with NOVA's 'Return to the Moon' documentary tonight (interview)
articles.description: The new Artemis 2 NOVA documentary "Return to the Moon" will air on PBS Wednesday (April 15) to offer a behind-the-scenes look at the moon mission.

articles.title: NASA's Artemis 2 moonshot was just the 'opening act' for America's return to the moon, space agency chief says
articles.description: Artemis 2 kicks off a lunar "relay race" that will lead the NASA and its international partners to a moon base and, eventually, Mars, NASA chief Jared Isaacman says.

articles.title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon shot just landed on Saturday Night Live — space toilet jokes and all (video)
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission wowed the world enough to earn a space spoof skit on Saturday Night Live one day after a perfect splashdown. Watch how it went.

articles.title: This Artemis 2 astronaut really loves Rise | Space photo of the day for April 14, 2026
articles.description: "Rise Wiseman" might be the most beloved mission mascot in history.

articles.title: Artemis 2 calling ISS! Watch the farthest-ever astronaut call from the moon to Earth (video)
articles.description: Astronauts aboard Artemis 2 and the ISS connected across a record-breaking Earth–moon distance in the farthest crew call ever made in space.

articles.title: How Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman saved the mission's moon mascot: 'It's hard not to love this little guy. I can't let Rise out of my sight'
articles.description: Rise joined the Artemis 2 crew as a mascot of the moon, bearing 5.6 million names. But the mascot quickly took on a new meaning.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts celebrate successful return to Earth | Space photo of the day for April 13, 2026
articles.description: Artemis 2's Victor Glover and Christina Koch are all smiles after splashdown.

articles.title: 'The most special thing that will ever happen in my life': Artemis 2 astronauts describe their epic moon mission
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts are back on Earth, and they've begun processing their historic moon mission. But it's still tough for them to put the experience into words.

articles.title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 205 — All About Artemis
articles.description: On Episode 205 of This Week In Space, Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik discuss the flight of Artemis 2.

articles.title: Artemis 3 and beyond: What's next for NASA after Artemis 2 moon success
articles.description: NASA doesn't plan to rest on its laurels after the historic success of its Artemis 2 moon mission. Here are the agency's ambitious plans for Earth's nearest neighbor.

articles.title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts came home today (April 10), wrapping up an epic mission that broke spaceflight records and set the stage for even more ambitious moonshots to come.

articles.title: The Artemis 2 astronauts saw a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon. Here's what it looked like
articles.description: A newly released NASA timelapse reveals the moon crossing the sun as seen from deep space, giving the Artemis 2 crew a rare extended view of totality and the solar corona.
